headline: Ireland probles Meta's Instagram, Facebook over EU manipulation concerns

Under the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA), the regulator can probe major online platforms and search engines ​headquartered in Ireland, and fine them up to 6% of global turnover for breaches of online content rules.  Coimisiun na Mean, the regulator, said the suspected breaches concern whether users can easily select and modify the content they see, and whether Facebook and Instagram's interfaces deceive ‌or manipulate them away from ⁠doing so.
